title: TotalEnergies Starts Permitting for $16bn Chilean Green Hydrogen Plant
content: TotalEnergiesâ€™ hydrogen subsidiary, in partnership with Eren through TotalEnergies H2, has initiated the permitting process for a $16 billion green hydrogen project in San Gregorio, Chile. The H2 Magallanes Project aims to produce up to 1.9 million tonnes of green ammonia annually, primarily for export, and is estimated to require around 2GW of electrolysis capacity. The project will span 72,000 hectares, with 4,000 hectares allocated for infrastructure, including wind turbines, electrolysis units, a desalination plant, and a port terminal. Environmental permitting may extend to 2027, with production beginning in 2030. Despite current investor caution in the green hydrogen space, the project leverages the region's strong wind resources and strategic location near the Strait of Magellan. This initiative aligns with Chileâ€™s goal of reaching 25GW of green hydrogen capacity by 2030.
author: Analysts Account
